DISCOVERY 610, PLAINTIFF SAN FRANCISCO PIER 33 LLC'S MOTION TO COMPEL THE DEPOSITION OF THE PORT OF SAN FRANCISCO IS DENIED. AFTER REVIEWING THE DECLARATION OF THE DIRECTOR OF THE PORT OF SAN FRANCISCO IN ITS ENTIRETY, THE COURT FINDS NEITHER AN EXPRESS NOR AN IMPLIED WAIVER OF THE PRIVILEGE BY THE SUBMISSION OF THE DECLARATION (SEE: SOUTHERN CALIFORNIA GAS COMPANY V. PUBLIC UTILITIES COMMISSION (1990) 50 CAL. 3D 31). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE CHAN. COURT REPORTER: CLAUDIA A. BETTUCCHI, CSF # 12214

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition Of The Port Of San Francisco
Off Calendar Jan-08-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Rachel Chatman, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: San Francisco Pier 33 LLC
Donal P. Margolis, Deputy City Attorney, for Defendant and opposing party: City and County of San Francisco

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted. Plaintiff San Francisco Pier 33 LLC's motion to compel the deposition of the port of San Francisco is denied. After reviewing the declaration of the director of the Port of San Francisco in its entirety, the Court finds neither an express nor an implied waiver of the privilege by the submission of the declaration (See: Southern California Gas Company v. Public Utilities Commission (1990) 50 Cal. 3d 31). Order signed in open court. Commissioner: Bruce Chan. Court Reporter: Claudia A. Bettucchi, CSF # 12214
